verdict_reasoning: MAP2K2 is a cytoplasmic serine/threonine kinase functioning in the intracellular Ras-MAPK signaling cascade. It lacks any membrane anchor (no transmembrane domain, GPI anchor, or lipidation signal) and is not secreted. Although it may transiently associate with membrane-proximal signaling scaffolds during activation, it remains a soluble cytoplasmic protein with no stable mechanism for reaching the outer leaflet of the plasma membrane.
